user_query:
I’m a single mom keeping track of my kids’ STEM learning at home. Please help me compile a mid-April progress update that I can share with them and use to adjust our schedule.

Inputs to use (all are in the workspace):
- input/goals.json
- input/time_log.csv
- all Markdown notes under input/notes/ (inspect the directory and include every *.md file there)

Produce two outputs:
1) output/progress_report.md — a clear, human-readable status report that includes:
   - A header with the reporting period set from the earliest and latest dates found in input/time_log.csv.
   - A brief overview paragraph summarizing total minutes logged per child for the period.
   - For each child (Maya and Leo):
     • Hours by subject: list each subject with its total minutes from input/time_log.csv (hours optional),
     • Goal progress: show total minutes logged vs the child’s total monthly goal minutes (sum of that child’s goal_minutes_by_subject from input/goals.json) and the overall progress percentage (round to the nearest whole percent),
     • Top 3 topics by time with their minutes (from input/time_log.csv; if fewer than 3 topics exist, list all; ties can be in any order),
     • Achievements: bullet points pulled verbatim from all input/notes/*.md files that mention that child under an “Achievements” section,
     • Challenges: bullet points pulled verbatim from all input/notes/*.md files that mention that child under a “Challenges” section,
     • Next steps (auto-generated): two bullets — (1) “Plan an extra 30-minute session on [lowest-progress subject by minutes vs its goal]” and (2) “Address: [the first Challenges bullet you listed for this child]”. If a child has no Challenges bullets, skip the second item.
   - A Sources section listing every input file you actually used by relative path.

2) output/summary.json — a structured summary with this schema:
   {
     "period": {"start": "YYYY-MM-DD", "end": "YYYY-MM-DD"},
     "files_inspected": ["input/time_log.csv", "input/goals.json", "input/notes/<each-notes-file>"],
     "children": [
       {
         "name": "<child>",
         "total_minutes": <int>,
         "goal_minutes_total": <int>,
         "goal_progress_percent": <int>,
         "minutes_by_subject": {"<subject>": <int>, ...},
         "top_topics": [{"topic": "<string>", "minutes": <int>}, ... up to 3],
         "achievements_count": <int>,
         "challenges_count": <int>
       }, ...
     ]
   }

Requirements and constraints:
- Use only the provided local files; do not fetch anything from the internet.
- Determine the reporting period by scanning dates in input/time_log.csv (not hard-coded).
- Treat minutes strictly as integers from input/time_log.csv. Compute per-child totals and per-subject totals from those rows. Compute each child’s goal_minutes_total by summing that child’s goal_minutes_by_subject in input/goals.json. Compute goal_progress_percent = round( (total_minutes / goal_minutes_total) * 100 ).
- For the notes: inspect every *.md file under input/notes/, extract the lines under “Achievements” and “Challenges” sections, and attribute them to the correct child based on the name at the start of each bullet (e.g., “Maya:” or “Leo:”).
- Make sure both output files are created exactly at the specified paths and that the counts and totals in output/summary.json are consistent with the report.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
